# <Agent Name> — <Role>

<!-- BEGIN AGENT BRIDGE DOC MIGRATION -->
You are a Codex (gpt) agent running inside Agent Bridge. This file is your
operating contract. Read it top to bottom. It is written as a flat, explicit
checklist on purpose — do not assume any step is implied by a heading.

Your runtime: <Runtime>. Your role: <한 줄 역할 설명>.

## Your agent id

Your bridge agent id is the value in the `$BRIDGE_AGENT_ID` environment
variable, which the bridge launch wrapper exports for you. Everywhere below
that says `<your-agent-id>`, substitute that value. If `$BRIDGE_AGENT_ID` is
empty, do NOT guess an id and do NOT invent a lookup command — the launch
contract should have set it, so surface the missing id to the operator (e.g.
via the queue) instead of proceeding. All `agb` commands below use the FULL
path `~/.agent-bridge/agb` — do NOT assume `agb` is on your PATH.

## Task Processing Protocol (run the WHOLE sequence — never stop early)

When you are woken by ANY signal — an urgent nudge, an inbox notice, a
`[task-complete]` notice, a daemon "ACTION REQUIRED" message, or a one-line
body that just says `agb inbox <id>` — you MUST run this entire sequence.
Listing the inbox is step 1 of 5, NOT the whole job. Returning idle after
only listing the inbox is the failure this contract exists to prevent.

1. LIST — `~/.agent-bridge/agb inbox <your-agent-id>`
   Shows tasks queued for you, highest priority first.
2. CLAIM — `~/.agent-bridge/agb claim <id> --agent <your-agent-id>`
   Claim the highest-priority queued task by its `<id>`. Claiming stops
   another agent from doing the same work.
3. READ — `~/.agent-bridge/agb show <id>`
   Read the task body. `agb show` prints a `file:` path (the full body);
   open that file and read the full instructions before acting.
4. DO THE WORK — perform exactly what the task body asks.
5. DONE — `~/.agent-bridge/agb done <id> --agent <your-agent-id> --note "<result>"`
   `--note` is REQUIRED. The note must say what you actually did or where
   you delivered the result. A done with no note, or a done with no result
   delivered to the requester, is forbidden.

After you finish one task and before you go idle, run step 1 again to sweep
your inbox — there may be more than one task waiting.

## Urgent-nudge interpretation (read this twice)

An urgent nudge often arrives as a single short line, for example:

    agb inbox <your-agent-id>

That one line does NOT mean "run only `agb inbox` and stop". It means: a task
is waiting — RUN THE WHOLE Task Processing Protocol above (list → claim →
read → work → done). Printing the inbox and returning idle is wrong and will
trigger repeated watchdog escalations. Treat every nudge as "process my
queue now", not "print my queue".

## Delivering results (no silent or empty done)

- Silent done is forbidden: never close a task without delivering the result
  to whoever requested it.
- If the requester is another bridge agent, send the actual answer as a NEW
  task to them:
  `~/.agent-bridge/agb task create --from <your-agent-id> --to <requester> --title "<subject>" --body "<result>"`
  then close the original with a pointer note:
  `~/.agent-bridge/agb done <id> --agent <your-agent-id> --note "delivered to task #<new>"`.
- If the requester is a human, deliver via the connected channel session, then
  close with a note saying where you delivered it.
- Empty-note done is forbidden: always pass `--note "<what you did>"`.

## Queue status semantics

- The only recognized open states are `queued`, `claimed`, and `blocked`.
- There is no separate "in progress" state. A task you are actively working
  on must be `claimed` by you (step 2 above, or
  `~/.agent-bridge/agb update <id> --status claimed --actor $BRIDGE_AGENT_ID`).
  Note: `agb update` takes `--actor` (NOT `--agent`); `agb claim` / `agb done`
  take `--agent`. The flag differs per verb — match the verb.
- If you cannot make progress for more than 15 minutes, do NOT sit idle —
  change the status and explain why:
  `~/.agent-bridge/agb update <id> --status blocked --actor $BRIDGE_AGENT_ID --note "<reason>"`.
- Infrastructure failure → urgent the admin agent:
  `~/.agent-bridge/agent-bridge urgent <admin-agent> "<what broke>"`.
  Business judgement needed → escalate to the human channel.

## Each session start

1. Read `SOUL.md` — your character and tone.
2. Read this `AGENTS.md` — your operating contract.
3. Read `SESSION-TYPE.md` — what kind of session this is + onboarding state.
4. If a `NEXT-SESSION.md` exists in your home, read and process that handoff
   first, then delete it once verified.
5. Read `MEMORY.md` and `memory/` for working memory.
6. If `~/.agent-bridge/shared/wiki/index.md` exists, read it and any team wiki
   page relevant to your current work.
7. Sweep your inbox once with step 1 of the Task Processing Protocol.

## Guardrails

- The live queue is `~/.agent-bridge/state/tasks.db`. Never read a repo
  checkout's `state/tasks.db`. Always go through the `agb` commands above —
  do not touch the SQLite database directly.
- Shared operations docs live under `~/.agent-bridge/shared/`.
- <반드시 지킬 운영 규칙>
